demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a highly adapted, brightly lit home; a neighbour is present tonight

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The lights went out in the whole street and I couldn't see my own hand in front of my face. I got so scared, I tripped over the rug in the hallway and went down, and I was sure I was having a stroke. The power's back on now but I'm shaking and my heart's still racing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She's an older lady with very poor eyesight; the blackout left her in total darkness, she fell, and she got into a terrible panic thinking it was a stroke — her neighbour called for advice about whether she needs to be seen tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 40 minutes ago, during a brief neighbourhood power outage
symptom_narrative.duration: the fright and the racing heart have eased a little now the lights are back, but she's still very shaken
symptom_narrative.character: sudden total darkness, could not see anything at all, became very disoriented, tripped on the hallway rug and fell, then a wave of panic and a pounding heart
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: at the time it was terrifying — she thought she was dying or having a stroke
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: fell in the hallway; no one part hurts in particular now
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the panic came on with the darkness and is settling as the lights returned
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: the lights coming back on and her neighbour being with her
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: darkness and moving around when she cannot see
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she says her eyes have been getting worse for years — everything is blurry, glare from headlights is blinding, and she has almost no side vision left; nights are the worst
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no previous falls like this; she is usually steady in her own well-lit home
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally independent at home as long as it is brightly lit; tonight she is frightened to stand up or walk

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: cloudy, worsening eyesight for years — she has been told she has cataracts
past_medical_history.how_managed: waiting to discuss surgery with the eye specialist

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: glaucoma with loss of side vision — under an eye specialist

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: eye drops for the pressure in her eyes, once a day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; a supportive neighbour checks on her and is with her now; very reliant on strong lighting to get around

objective_data_offered.value: elderly woman sitting rigidly on the sofa clutching her neighbour's arm, visibly anxious and tearful, breathing fast, but talking clearly and knows where she is and what day it is

neighbour says she tripped on a loose hallway rug when the lights went out, sat straight back up, is moving both arms and both legs normally, has no obvious cuts, bumps to the head or bleeding, and did not black out
neighbour reports she is calming down now the lights are back and her heart is not pounding as hard as it was
both eyes look cloudy/greyish over the pupils on the video
loose rug in the hallway where she fell; hallway is dim with little contrast between the walls and the floor
